title: Full vs Lite Model Comparison
text: multimix package provides two model variants differ random effects structure:

title: Full vs Lite Model Comparison
text: Two independent random effects u1,u2∼N(0,σ12),N(0,σ22)u_1, u_2 \sim N(0, \sigma_1^2), N(0, \sigma_2^2) u1u_1 scales early phase, u2u_2 scales late phase independently Uses 2D Gauss-Hermite quadrature numerical integration 12 parameters (fixing): β01,β02,a1,a2,σ1,σ2,t1/2early,t1/2late,ηearly,ηlate,γearly,γlate\beta_{01}, \beta_{02}, a_1, a_2, \sigma_1, \sigma_2, t_{1/2}^{early}, t_{1/2}^{late}, \eta^{early}, \eta^{late}, \gamma^{early}, \gamma^{late}

title: Full vs Lite Model Comparison
text: Single shared random effect u∼N(0,σ2)u \sim N(0, \sigma^2) uu scales phases (via different coefficients a1,a2a_1, a_2) Uses 1D Gauss-Hermite quadrature (faster) 11 parameters (fixing): full model single σ\sigma instead σ1,σ2\sigma_1, \sigma_2

title: Full vs Lite Model Comparison
text: full model always log-likelihood least high lite model (since flexible model). However, improvement small, lite model may preferred parsimony.
code:
# Fit both models full_model <- multimix(sample_data, verbose = FALSE) lite_model <- multimix_lite(sample_data, verbose = FALSE)  # Compare log-likelihoods cat("Full model logLik:", full_model$logLik, "
") cat("Lite model logLik:", lite_model$logLik, "
") cat("Difference:", full_model$logLik - lite_model$logLik, "
")

title: Full vs Lite Model Comparison
text: full model, σ1\sigma_1 σ2\sigma_2 represent standard deviation -subject variation early late phases respectively. large σ1\sigma_1 small σ2\sigma_2 suggests subjects differ mainly early phase response.

title: Full vs Lite Model Comparison
text: lite model, single σ\sigma captures overall -subject variation. coefficients a1a_1 a2a_2 determine much variation expressed phase.

title: Full vs Lite Model Comparison
text: Look : Subject-level curves: spread full model suggests two random effects capture additional variability Population average: similar models data well-behaved Fit observed data: Compare closely points (empirical proportions) track blue line
code: library(ggplot2)  p_full <- plot(full_model) + ggtitle("Full Model (two random effects)") p_lite <- plot(lite_model) + ggtitle("Lite Model (single random effect)")  print(p_full) print(p_lite)

title: Full vs Lite Model Comparison
text: large datasets, consider starting lite model get reasonable initial parameter estimates, using inform bounds full model.

title: Introduction to multimix
text: multimix package fits Biphasic Temporal Decomposition Logistic Mixed Effects Models longitudinal binary outcome data. model designed settings probability event changes time two distinct phases — early phase late phase — subject-level variation captured random effects.

title: Introduction to multimix
Introduction to multimix
text: Input data must long format three required columns: Subject_ID: unique identifier subject Time: numeric time measurement (e.g., months post-operative) Binary_outcome: binary (0/1) outcome time point Let’s inspect data structure:
code:
data(sample_data) head(sample_data) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   Subject_ID   Time Binary_outcome #>        <dbl>  <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1          1 0.03                1 #> 2          1 0.0623              1 #> 3          1 0.0633              1 #> 4          1 0.0945              1 #> 5          1 0.263               1 #> 6          1 0.43                1 cat("Subjects:", length(unique(sample_data$Subject_ID)), "
") #> Subjects: 44 cat("Observations:", nrow(sample_data), "
") #> Observations: 4743 cat("Time range:", range(sample_data$Time), "
") #> Time range: 0.03 142.6967 cat("Outcome prevalence:", mean(sample_data$Binary_outcome), "
") #> Outcome prevalence: 0.2960152

title: Introduction to multimix
text: multimix() function fits full model two independent random effects (one temporal phase). uses multiple random initializations find global optimum. Key arguments: fixed_pars: named list parameters hold fixed (default: list(beta0_2 = 0)) max_tries: number random initializations attempt (default: 20) seed: random seed reproducibility (default: 1234) verbose: print progress messages (default: FALSE)

title: Introduction to multimix
text: print method displays estimated parameters readable names: β₀₁, β₀₂: baseline log-odds early late phases ₁, ₂: random effect scaling coefficients σ₁, σ₂: random effect standard deviations t½: half-life temporal function η, γ: shape parameters temporal decomposition

title: Introduction to multimix
text: plot shows: Grey dashed lines: subject-level fitted probability trajectories Blue solid line: population average trajectory Points: observed empirical proportions time quantiles

title: Introduction to multimix
text: faster fitting data limited, multimix_lite() uses single shared random effect across phases:
code: model_lite <- multimix_lite(sample_data, max_tries = 20, verbose = FALSE) print(model_lite) plot(model_lite)

title: Introduction to multimix
text: can supply custom initialization bounds guide optimizer:
code: my_lower <- lower_bounds_example my_upper <- upper_bounds_example  # Narrow the search for half-life parameters my_lower["log_t_half_early"] <- log(0.5) my_upper["log_t_half_early"] <- log(5)  model_custom <- multimix(   sample_data,   lower_bounds = my_lower,   upper_bounds = my_upper,   max_tries = 30 )

title: Understanding Temporal Decomposition
text: multimix model decomposes conditional odds binary outcome two temporal components: Conditional odds(t)=exp(β01+a1u1)⋅Λ1(t)+exp(β02+a2u2)⋅Λ2(t) \text{Conditional odds}(t) = \exp(\beta_{01} + a_1 u_1) \cdot \Lambda_1(t) + \exp(\beta_{02} + a_2 u_2) \cdot \Lambda_2(t) Λ1(t)\Lambda_1(t) captures early phase (density function g(t)g(t)) Λ2(t)\Lambda_2(t) captures late phase (hazard function h(t)=g(t)/(1−G(t))h(t) = g(t) / (1 - G(t))). functions derived generalized temporal decomposition parameterized : Half-life (t1/2t_{1/2}): controls time scale Eta (η\eta, called nu internally): exponent time Gamma (γ\gamma, called m internally): shape distribution

title: Understanding Temporal Decomposition
text: Let’s visualize early late phase functions behave different parameter settings.

title: Understanding Temporal Decomposition
text: half-life parameter shifts temporal scale: shorter half-lives produce faster-decaying early phases.
code: times <- seq(0.1, 20, by = 0.1)  df_thalf <- data.frame(   Time = rep(times, 3),   Value = c(     get_early_phase(times, thalf = 1, eta = 1, gamma = 0),     get_early_phase(times, thalf = 3, eta = 1, gamma = 0),     get_early_phase(times, thalf = 8, eta = 1, gamma = 0)   ),   HalfLife = rep(c("t½ = 1", "t½ = 3", "t½ = 8"), each = length(times)) )  ggplot(df_thalf, aes(x = Time, y = Value, color = HalfLife)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   labs(     title = "Early Phase (Density) — Effect of Half-Life",     x = "Time", y = "g(t)", color = "Half-Life"   ) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

title: Understanding Temporal Decomposition
text: Higher eta values produce sharper, peaked density curves.
code: df_eta <- data.frame(   Time = rep(times, 3),   Value = c(     get_early_phase(times, thalf = 3, eta = 0.5, gamma = 0),     get_early_phase(times, thalf = 3, eta = 1, gamma = 0),     get_early_phase(times, thalf = 3, eta = 3, gamma = 0)   ),   Eta = rep(c("η = 0.5", "η = 1", "η = 3"), each = length(times)) )  ggplot(df_eta, aes(x = Time, y = Value, color = Eta)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   labs(     title = "Early Phase (Density) — Effect of Eta",     x = "Time", y = "g(t)", color = "Eta"   ) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

title: Understanding Temporal Decomposition
text: early phase (density) peaks declines, late phase (hazard) increases time — reflecting intuition different mechanisms drive outcome different time scales.
code: df_phases <- data.frame(   Time = rep(times, 2),   Value = c(     get_early_phase(times, thalf = 3, eta = 1, gamma = 0),     get_late_phase(times, thalf = 3, eta = 1, gamma = 0)   ),   Phase = rep(     c("Early (density g(t))", "Late (hazard h(t))"), each = length(times)   ) )  ggplot(df_phases, aes(x = Time, y = Value, color = Phase)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   labs(     title = "Early vs Late Phase Components",     x = "Time", y = "Value", color = "Phase"   ) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +   coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 2))

title: Understanding Temporal Decomposition
text: decompos() function handles six parameter sign combinations: case γ<0\gamma < 0 η<0\eta < 0 mathematically undefined produce error.

title: Understanding Temporal Decomposition
text: Start eta = 1, gamma = 0 (exponential-like decay) initial exploration Use default bounds starting point narrow based domain knowledge half-life parameter interpretable: controls phase peaks transitions optimizer struggles converge, try fixing gamma parameters 0 via fixed_pars

what: Biphasic Temporal Decomposition Logistic Mixed Effects Model
title: Biphasic Temporal Decomposition Logistic Mixed Effects Model
text: multimix R package fitting visualizing Biphasic Temporal Decomposition Logistic Mixed Effects Model — framework analyzing longitudinal binary outcomes exhibit two temporal phases (early late) subject-specific random effects.

title: Biphasic Temporal Decomposition Logistic Mixed Effects Model
text: model decomposes conditional odds binary outcome early late temporal components: : - Λ₁(t) Λ₂(t) generalized temporal decomposition functions early late phases - u₁, u₂ subject-specific random effects (single shared u lite variant) - Parameters estimated via maximum likelihood Gauss-Hermite quadrature Two model variants available: - multimix() — Full model two independent random effects per subject - multimix_lite() — Lighter model single shared random effect
code: Conditional odds = exp(β₀₁ + a₁·u₁) · Λ₁(t) + exp(β₀₂ + a₂·u₂) · Λ₂(t)

title: Biphasic Temporal Decomposition Logistic Mixed Effects Model
text: Install development version GitHub:

title: Biphasic Temporal Decomposition Logistic Mixed Effects Model
code: library(multimix)  # Load example data data(sample_data) head(sample_data)  # Fit the full model model <- multimix(sample_data)  # View estimated parameters print(model)  # Visualize fitted trajectories plot(model)

title: Biphasic Temporal Decomposition Logistic Mixed Effects Model
code: # Fit the lighter single-random-effect variant model_lite <- multimix_lite(sample_data)  print(model_lite) plot(model_lite)

title: Temporal decomposition function — decompos
text: Computes cumulative distribution (capgt), density (gt), hazard (ht) generalized temporal decomposition model parameterized half-life, exponent nu (eta), shape m (gamma).

title: Temporal decomposition function — decompos
Temporal decomposition function — decompos
text: time Numeric vector time values. thalf Half-life parameter temporal function. nu Exponent time (eta model notation). m Exponent entire function (gamma model notation). complet Integer flag complete (1) vs incomplete (0) distribution (currently unused).

title: Temporal decomposition function — decompos
text: list components: capgt (cumulative), gt (density/early phase), ht (hazard/late phase).

title: Temporal decomposition function — decompos
text: Handles 6 valid parameter combinations based signs m nu. case m < 0 nu < 0 undefined raise error.

Columns : Subject_ID, Time, Binary_outcome fixed_pars named list parameters fix values (.e. optimization ) lower_bounds named list lower bounds guess initial params within upper_bounds named list upper bounds guess initial params within max_tries number initial params tried return_first_success logical. TRUE first model works returned. Otherwise exhaust full number retries find optimal solution. return_first_sucess Deprecated. Use return_first_success instead. verbose logical. TRUE error messages displayed failed attempt seed random number generator seed

title: multimix 1.0.0.9000 (development)
text: Added multimix_lite(): lighter model variant single shared random effect per subject, suitable smaller datasets faster convergence. Added plot() methods multimix_model multimix_model_lite objects, producing temporal decomposition probability curves. Added print() method multimix_model objects. Added sample_data dataset examples testing. Added generate_random_init_from_bounds() helper random initialization within parameter bounds. Added decompos() computing temporal decomposition function. Added check_df_long() validating input data format.

title: multimix 1.0.0.9000 (development)
text: Gauss-Hermite quadrature used numerical integration likelihood evaluation. L-BFGS-B optimization user-configurable bounds initial values. Empirical Bayes estimates subject-specific random effects computed post-fitting. CI via GitHub Actions: R-CMD-check, lintr, test coverage workflows. Full documentation roxygen2, including parameter tables examples.

title: multimix 1.0.0
text: Initial release.
